description: Catalog of major public AI training datasets + how to check if your content is in them. Some support direct query (Common Crawl CDX); others require manual walkthroughs or third-party services (Have I Been Trained).

common-crawl.notes: The single most important public dataset. Backbone of almost every modern LLM. CDX API lets you query if a specific URL was crawled and when. Opt-out: block CCBot in robots.txt, but this only stops FUTURE crawls.

howToCheck.description: HTTP GET with url + snapshot parameters returns JSON records of every crawled version

steps: Edit your site's robots.txt (or configure via CMS)

Add these lines:
    User-agent: CCBot
    Disallow: /
Deploy. Future Common Crawl runs will skip your site.
Request removal of existing snapshots: email support@commoncrawl.org with your domain
optOutWalkthrough.verification: After next monthly crawl, query CDX API — your URLs should not appear in new snapshots. Existing snapshots cannot be retroactively removed from downloaded copies already in circulation.
optOutWalkthrough.caveat: Common Crawl is a nonprofit archive. Removing from it doesn't remove from the many copies mirrored across research institutions and already in LLM training sets.

have-i-been-trained.notes: LAION-5B is the public image-text dataset used to train the major open image models. Have I Been Trained (by Spawning AI) provides a search UI + opt-out registry. Major commercial image models (Stable Diffusion XL, etc.) honor the HIBT opt-out list.

howToCheck.description: Web UI search: upload photos or search by URL to find matches in LAION-5B

steps: Open haveibeentrained.com
Upload a photo OR paste the URL of an image you want to check
Results show: match confidence + which LAION subset + original source URL
For bulk search: create account and use batch upload (free)
checkWalkthrough.verification: Match list shows found/not-found per image.

steps: Open haveibeentrained.com → 'Opt Out' section
Upload images you want excluded (up to 50 per batch)
Register your account / claim ownership
Opt-out registry is honored by SD 3, Stable Audio, Adobe Firefly + growing list of image models
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Registry confirmation email. Note: images already in training sets for older models (SD 1.5, SD 2.1, DALL-E 2) cannot be removed retroactively.

the-pile.notes: Includes PubMed, ArXiv, Github, Stack Exchange, Common Crawl subset, Reddit (via Pushshift), Wikipedia, books, etc. Your Reddit posts pre-2022 are almost certainly in there via the Pushshift subset. No opt-out mechanism — it's a frozen dataset.

howToCheck.description: No public query API. To check: download the relevant subset (e.g., Pushshift Reddit dump ~200 GB) and grep for your username. Research-only feasibility.

steps: If you posted on Reddit, Stack Exchange, PubMed, ArXiv, or GitHub before 2022 — assume you're in it
No URL query: dataset is downloadable only from pile.eleuther.ai
The Pile hosting was suspended in 2024 due to DMCA complaints but widely mirrored
Practical check: search the source platform (Reddit username on pushshift.io archive) — if present on source, likely in Pile

c4.notes: Derived from Common Crawl with filtering. Opt-out from Common Crawl (see above) propagates to future C4 versions. Washington Post + Allen AI published a C4 search tool in 2023 letting you query domain inclusion.

howToCheck.description: C4 Search tool by Allen AI: https://c4-search.apps.allenai.org/

steps: Open c4-search.apps.allenai.org
Enter your domain (e.g., yourwebsite.com)
Search returns: how many tokens from your domain are in C4 + sample pages
Results rank domains by token count — see where you rank
checkWalkthrough.verification: Domain-level results with token count + sample snippets.

steps: Block CCBot in robots.txt (same as Common Crawl opt-out)
C4 is derived from Common Crawl — upstream opt-out propagates
No direct path to remove from existing C4 copies
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Next C4 build after your robots.txt change won't include your new content.
optOutWalkthrough.caveat: Existing C4 (2019 + 2023) remains in every model trained on it. Cannot be retroactively removed.

openai-webtext.notes: Originally 8M documents linked from Reddit submissions with ≥3 karma, pre-2017. Not publicly released. OpenWebText (EleutherAI's open replication) is public. If your site was linked from popular Reddit posts 2015-2017, you're in one or both.

howToCheck.description: Not directly queryable. Heuristic: if your site has pages that went viral on Reddit pre-2017, likely included.

steps: Check if any of your URLs had Reddit submissions with ≥3 karma before Nov 2017
Use camas.github.io/reddit-search or pushshift.io to search Reddit history
If found: you're in WebText (OpenAI proprietary) + likely OpenWebText (public replication)

redpajama.notes: Recreation of Llama's training data using only public sources. Mostly Common Crawl + GitHub + Wikipedia + Books3. Robots.txt opt-out from Common Crawl blocks future RedPajama builds.

howToCheck.description: No search tool. Check sources individually (Common Crawl + GitHub + Wikipedia).

steps: Inherits from Common Crawl — check CC presence first
Also check GitHub public commits, Wikipedia edits (attributable), Books3 (removed post-DMCA)
RedPajama v2 expanded to 30T tokens — more likely to include lower-traffic sites

dolma.notes: AI2's open 3T-token corpus. Powers OLMo (open-source LLM). Heavy Common Crawl dependency + GitHub + Wikipedia + peS2o (Semantic Scholar papers). One of the most transparent datasets in existence.

howToCheck.description: AI2 Dolma has a WIMBD (What's In My Big Data) search tool.

steps: Open wimbd.apps.allenai.org
Search for your domain, name, or text snippet
Tool shows frequency + sample contexts across multiple datasets (Dolma, C4, RedPajama, Pile)
checkWalkthrough.verification: Count + sample context shown per dataset.

steps: Block CCBot in robots.txt
For GitHub data: make repos private or delete (GitHub honors removal)
For Semantic Scholar: request removal via email
optOutWalkthrough.verification: Future Dolma builds won't include newly blocked content.

fineweb.notes: HuggingFace's highly-filtered Common Crawl derivative (2024). The cleanest large-scale open dataset. Widely adopted for new open-source model training.

howToCheck.description: Can be searched via HuggingFace datasets API (technical).

steps: Install huggingface-cli + datasets library
Load FineWeb as streaming dataset and grep for your domain
Only practical for researchers — most users should assume inclusion if site is in Common Crawl
Block CCBot in robots.txt
Future FineWeb builds will exclude your site
optOutWalkthrough.caveat: Existing FineWeb distributions cannot be retroactively filtered.
